codeReview.summary: Implementation covers required formats and priority chain, but single-quote recovery corrupts escaped apostrophes in argument strings, so extracted arguments are not faithfully preserved.

issues.description: _fix_single_quotes replaces every single quote outside double-quoted strings, including escaped apostrophes within single-quoted values. For input <tool_call>{'name':'say','arguments':{'text':'it\'s ok'}}</tool_call>, parse_tool_calls returns arguments {'text': 'it"s ok'} instead of preserving "it's ok", violating structured extraction fidelity.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The python-worker skill prescribes test-first (Step 3), but the transcript shows implementation file creation before test creation while handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure is true. Procedure compliance criteria appear inconsistent.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for workerSessionId ac4d778e-b5e3-4b9b-91e3-33b930041c51 shows Create mtb/quality_benchmarks/tool_call_parser.py before Create tests/test_tool_call_parser.py; handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true.

summary: Reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, skill, and commit 3f5afab for feature tool-call-parser. Found one blocking correctness bug in single-quote normalization that mutates escaped apostrophes in parsed arguments; feature should be fixed before acceptance.
